purpose: Serve and validate a structured OpenAPI manifest so humans and agents can discover API capabilities without source inspection.

endpoints.description: Validate an OpenAPI document structure before publication.
Return the current OpenAPI document or manifest metadata.

dependencies.purpose: Validate OpenAPI documents and surface structural errors

sidecar_specification.purpose: OpenAPI manifest serving and validation for API discoverability.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when an API should expose machine-readable capability metadata to clients, agents, and test harnesses.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a replacement for runtime auth, business validation, or network traffic inspection.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Build an OpenAPI document. 2) POST it to /openapi/validate during CI. 3) Publish or serve it from /openapi/manifest when valid.

failure_modes.condition: Document is structurally invalid or missing required OpenAPI fields
failure_modes.recovery: Correct the document before publishing it to clients or agents

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use SwaggerParser.validate on the supplied document and return flattened error messages. Keep /openapi/manifest read-only and deterministic.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: OpenAPI schemas can pass JSON Schema checks but still violate specification rules. Preserve the original document structure when serving it.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Separate validation from serving so CI and runtime can use the same module.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not generate manifests by inspecting live traffic and do not fetch remote references during validation.
